name: Pratie Cakes
recipeIngredient: 2 cups Potatoes (mashed, fresh or prepared from flakes)
1/2 cup All-purpose flour (unsifted) - reserve some for dusting the board
recipeInstructions:
1. Transfer mashed potatoes to a large bowl.
2. Add flour, salt, and onion. Mix thoroughly with hands until well combined and smooth.
3. Pat mixture onto a lightly floured board to form a rectangle about 1/2 inch thick.
4. Using a 3-inch cookie cutter, cut the dough into individual pieces.
5. Place the cut pieces on a baking sheet covered with waxed paper.
6. Refrigerate until ready to cook (about 30 minutes).
7. To cook, melt 1 tablespoon of margarine in a large frying pan or stovetop griddle over moderately hot heat.
8. Fry the cakes one at a time, turning them once they are golden brown on both sides.
9. Serve immediately.
Food exchange per serving: 1 STARCH EXCHANGE + 1 FAT EXCHANGE CHO: 15g; PRO: 2g; FAT: 5g; CAL: 110
Low-sodium diets: Omit salt from the mashed potato mixture and recipe as needed.
Certain Irish folk refer to these potato cakes as "praties". They are delicious! Mix and shape ahead, then chill before cooking.
